ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-05-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS MANOJ SHRESTHA AND YELLOW CAB COOPERATIVE, INC. DBA YELLOW CAB COMANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF MAY NOT SEEK PUNITIVE DAMAGES. DEFENDANTS MET THEIR INITIAL BURDEN BY PRODUCING EVIDENCE ESTABLISHING THAT THE DRIVER WAS NOT ACTING WITH MALICE, OPPRESSION, OR FRAUD. (DEFENDANTS' UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACT "UMF" NOS. 1-4.) PLAINTIFF HAS NOT RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. FIRST, ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF ARGUES THAT THE DRIVER ACTED INTENTIONALLY, THE COMPLAINT ONLY STATES CAUSES OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE. THE PLEADINGS SERVE AS THE "OUTER MEASURE OF MATERIALITY" IN A SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION, AND THE MOTION MAY NOT BE GRANTED OR DENIED ON ISSUES NOT RAISED BY THE PLEADINGS. (LAABS V. CITY OF VICTORVILLE (2008) 163 CAL.APP.4TH 1242, 1258.) IN ANY EVENT, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED ANY EVIDENCE FROM WHICH A JURY COULD REASONABLY CONCLUDE BY CLEAR AND CONVINCING EVIDENCE THAT THE DRIVER INTENDED TO INJURE PLAINTIFF. SECOND, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT INTRODUCED EVIDENCE ESTABLISHING THAT THE DRIVER WAS OTHERWISE ACTING WITH MALICE, OPPRESSION, OR FRAUD. THE CASE CITED IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT THAT THE DRIVER ACTED WITH A CONSCIOUS DISREGARD OF THE SAFETY OF OTHERS, PETERSON V. SUPERIOR COURT (1982) 31 CAL.3D 147, IS FACTUALLY DISTINGUISHABLE. IN PETERSON, THERE WERE ALLEGATIONS THAT THE DRIVER OF THE VEHICLE WAS DRUNK. HERE, IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT THE DRIVER WAS USING A LEGAL, HANDS-FREE BLUETOOTH DEVICE. (UMF NO. 4.) THIS IS INSUFFICIENT TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER THE DRIVER WAS ACTING WITH A CONSCIOUS DISREGARD OF THE SAFETY OF OTHERS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-25-2014 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION And Notice Of Motion
Off Calendar Jun-05-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS MANOJ SHRESTHA and YELLOW CAB COOPERATIVE, INC. DBA YELLOW CAB COMANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is granted. Plaintiff may not seek punitive damages. Defendants met their initial burden by producing evidence establishing that the driver was not acting with malice, oppression, or fraud. (Defendants' Undisputed Material Fact "UMF" nos. 1-4.) Plaintiff has not raised a triable issue of material fact. First, although Plaintiff argues that the driver acted intentionally, the complaint only states causes of action for negligence. The pleadings serve as the "outer measure of materiality" in a summary judgment motion, and the motion may not be granted or denied on issues not raised by the pleadings. (Laabs v. City of Victorville (2008) 163 Cal.App.4th 1242, 1258.) In any event, Plaintiff has not presented any evidence from which a jury could reasonably conclude by clear and convincing evidence that the driver intended to injure Plaintiff. Second, Plaintiff has not introduced evidence establishing that the driver was otherwise acting with malice, oppression, or fraud. The case cited in support of Plaintiff's argument that the driver acted with a conscious disregard of the safety of others, Peterson v. Superior Court (1982) 31 Cal.3d 147, is factually distinguishable. In Peterson, there were allegations that the driver of the vehicle was drunk. Here, it is undisputed that the driver was using a legal, hands-free Bluetooth device. (UMF no. 4.) This is insufficient to raise a triable issue of material fact as to whether the driver was acting with a conscious disregard of the safety of others. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION And Notice Of Motion
Off Calendar May-30-2014 Continued To Jun-05-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs MANOJ SHRESTHA, YELLOW CAB COOPERATIVE, INC. DBA YELLOW CAB MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is continued to June 5, 2014 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than May 30, 2014 at 4:00 p.m. or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving papers. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-24-2013 Set For Trial On Jun-30-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
